stereotype: «ResourceAccess» — owns artifactStore (content-addressable git-backed storage for Phase-3 construction outputs)
volatility: The content-addressable store for Phase-3 construction outputs — opaque, byte-shaped build products (source trees, compiled artifacts, helm charts, k8s manifests, deployable bundles) that the construction pipeline emits and operations consumes. The backing store (git provider / Gitea today; managed GitHub/GitLab/Gitea Cloud or a Merkle-tree blob store tomorrow) is the encapsulated variable.

ops.signature: storeConstructionOutput(content: ConstructionOutput, idempotencyKey: fwra.IdempotencyKey) → contentAddress: string
ops.stereotype: Activity-wrapped write — idempotent on content; same content returns same address
ops.note: Persist a Phase-3 construction output into the content-addressable store and return its opaque content address (commit SHA or OID)

fields.name: Content
fields.note: ConstructionOutput struct { Bytes []byte; MIMEType string } — opaque Phase-3 build product

fields.name: IdempotencyKey
fields.note: caller-supplied, stable per logical write; derived from workflowId:activityId by the Manager

fields.name: ContentAddress
fields.note: canonical printable content-addressable identity (git commit SHA or tree/blob OID); plain string, not a wrapper type

ops.stereotype: Activity-wrapped read — trivially idempotent pure read
ops.note: Retrieve the construction output bytes addressed by a previously returned content address

fields.name: ContentAddress
fields.note: previously returned by storeConstructionOutput or appearing in an OutputTree

ops.stereotype: Activity-wrapped read — trivially idempotent pure read of tree snapshot
ops.note: Retrieve the flat map of logical-path → content-address entries rooted at the given tree-root content address (for operationsManager bundle materialisation)

fields.name: ContentAddress
fields.note: tree-root content address; e.g. deployable-bundle root returned by a prior storeConstructionOutput

fields.name: Entries
fields.note: flat path→entry-content-address map; OutputPath = string (logical slash-separated)

dataContracts: ConstructionOutput = { Bytes []byte; MIMEType string } // opaque Phase-3 build product
OutputTree = { Root string; Entries map[OutputPath]string } // flat path→address snapshot
OutputPath = string // logical slash-separated infrastructure-opaque path
fwra.IdempotencyKey // referenced from framework-go/resourceaccess; not redefined here
fwra.Error { Kind Kind; Retryable bool; Detail string; Cause error } // shared RA error model; kinds used: NotFound, Auth, Conflict, Transient, Infrastructure, ContractMisuse
errorModel: framework-go/resourceaccess.Error with kinds: Transient (retryable), Infrastructure (retryable), Conflict (retryable), Auth (terminal), NotFound (terminal), ContractMisuse (terminal). Retryable seeded on construction, never inferred at call site.
idempotency: storeConstructionOutput: content-addressable — identical content always returns the same address; idempotencyKey carried in infrastructure commit message for retry recognition. retrieveConstructionOutput / retrieveOutputTree: pure reads, trivially idempotent.

revisions.summary: Initial design with storeArtifact/retrieveArtifact/fetchTree over ArtifactRefSpec(ProjectID,ArtifactKind,Variant) and opaque ArtifactRef struct
Temporal removed from contract surface; idempotencyKey added as plain caller-supplied param; zero-Temporal discipline established

Scope narrowed to Phase-3 construction outputs only; ArtifactRefSpec and ArtifactRef deleted; content address is now a plain string; ops renamed to construction business verbs (storeConstructionOutput/retrieveConstructionOutput/retrieveOutputTree); systemDesignManager and projectDesignManager callers removed

SENIOR REVIEW PASSED — verdict pass, no contract-surface amendments; construction-verb rename endorsed; 3 follow-ups captured (IdempotencyKey→framework-go, ADR-X001 prose, DSL edge labels). Contract frozen.

revisions.byActivity: D-AA amendment (git target is now profile-selected)
revisions.summary: Amendment noting ArtifactTarget volatility: backing git target is now per-deployment-profile (cloud=Gitea, local=on-disk repo, enterprise=customer-hosted). Contract surface unchanged; §6 infrastructure-mapping table gains additional instances.
